title: content::process_content O(n*m) string rebuilds
description:
process_content in src/content.rs (lines 30-127) performs find-and-replace on the result string one match at a time. Each replacement rebuilds the entire string via format!("{}{}{}", &result[..pos], linked, &result[pos + len..]). For N matches in a string of length M, this is O(N*M) total allocation+copy work.

With many hashtags, mentions, and bare URLs in a single post, this compounds. Each replacement also shifts all subsequent match positions, meaning find() on later iterations may silently skip or double-match.

What goes wrong: posts with 20+ hashtags or many inline URLs will do 20+ full-string copies. More critically, the position-shifting means replacements can corrupt output (e.g., a URL that partially overlaps a hashtag match).

Fix: accumulate replacements as (start, end, replacement) spans, then build the final string in a single pass from left to right.
